title: Restricted access container
abstract: A restricted access container may include an outer shell and an inner tray. The outer shell may define a recess. The outer shell may include a first wall, a second wall, a third wall, and an opening. The first wall may include a hole and a notch. The second wall may be opposite the first wall. The third wall may be coupled to each of the first wall and the second wall. The opening may be opposite the third wall. The inner tray may define a receiving area. The inner tray may include a tab, a first protrusion, and a second protrusion. The first protrusion may be disposed on the tab. The inner tray may be inserted through the opening into the recess of the outer shell such that the first protrusion is disposed within the hole of the first wall to fix the inner tray to the outer shell and the second protrusion is disposed within the notch.
claims: 1. A restricted access container, comprising: an outer shell defining a recess, the outer shell including: a first wall including a first notch and a second notch defined thereby, the first notch and the second notch each having a closed end pointing toward each other relative to the first wall and an open end pointing away from each other relative to the first wall; a second wall opposite the first wall; a third wall extending between the first wall and the second wall; and an opening opposite the third wall, the opening defined by the first wall, the second wall, a fourth wall extending from the first wall to the second wall and a fifth wall extending from the first wall to the second wall; and an inner tray defining a receiving area, the inner tray including: a tab extending from the inner tray and moveable relative thereto; a first protrusion disposed on the tab; and a second protrusion disposed on the inner tray, wherein the inner tray is inserted through the opening into the recess of the outer shell such that the second protrusion is disposed within the second notch and the first protrusion is disposed within the first notch of the first wall to fix the inner tray relative to the outer shell in a locked position, wherein the first protrusion is pushed into the recess of the outer shell in an unlocked position, and wherein pulling the second protrusion away from second notch in the unlocked position slides the inner tray out of the outer shell.
